title: Living in Enfield: Council Tax, Schools, Transport and Local Services Guide
excerpt: A comprehensive guide to practical life in the London Borough of Enfield, covering council tax bands, school options, transport links, and essential local services.
metaDescription: Complete guide to living in Enfield. Council tax rates, best schools, transport links from EN1, NHS services, and local amenities across the borough.
content:
A Practical Guide to Living in Enfield

Whether you're considering a move to the London Borough of Enfield or you're a long-standing resident wanting to understand the services available to you, this guide covers the practical aspects of daily life in this diverse North London borough. From council tax and schools to transport and healthcare, here's everything you need to know about living in Enfield.

Council Tax in Enfield

How Council Tax Works

Council tax in Enfield is set annually by the London Borough of Enfield in conjunction with the Greater London Authority. The amount you pay depends on your property's council tax band, which was determined by the property's value in April 1991. Enfield currently has properties across all bands from A to H, though the majority fall within bands C to E.

Current Rates and Bands

Enfield's council tax rates are broadly in line with the outer London average. For the current financial year, a Band D property in Enfield pays approximately £1,800-£1,900 in total council tax (including the GLA precept). Properties in higher bands pay proportionally more — a Band H property pays roughly double the Band D rate. Properties in lower bands pay less, with Band A properties paying approximately two-thirds of the Band D rate.

Council Tax by Neighbourhood

Council tax bands vary significantly across the borough, broadly reflecting property values. Many properties in Edmonton fall within bands B to D, reflecting the area's more affordable housing stock. Palmers Green and Enfield Town properties typically fall within bands C to E. The larger, more valuable homes in Southgate, Winchmore Hill, and Cockfosters frequently fall within bands E to H, with band H properties attracting annual bills exceeding £3,500.

Discounts and Exemptions

Several discounts and exemptions are available. Single-person households receive a 25% discount. Full-time students are exempt. Properties left empty due to the owner's death are exempt for up to six months after probate is granted — relevant for executors managing inherited Enfield properties. Disabled residents may qualify for a reduction in their band. The council also offers a council tax support scheme for residents on low incomes.

Schools in Enfield

Primary Schools

The London Borough of Enfield has a wide range of primary schools, including community schools, faith schools, and academies. Several are rated Outstanding by Ofsted, including schools across different parts of the borough. The admissions process for community schools is based primarily on distance from the school, which creates the well-known 'catchment area' effect on property values — particularly in areas like Southgate and Winchmore Hill where schools are in high demand.

Secondary Schools

Enfield offers a diverse secondary school landscape. The borough is one of a small number in London that retains selective grammar schools — Enfield Grammar School and Latymer School both attract applications from across North London and beyond. Comprehensive secondary schools include Highlands School in Enfield Town, Winchmore School in Winchmore Hill, and Kingsmead School in Enfield Highway. The mix of selective and comprehensive provision gives families significant choice.

School Performance

Overall, Enfield schools perform well, with GCSE results improving year on year. The grammar schools consistently rank among the highest-performing schools in London. Among comprehensive schools, several achieve above-average results and provide excellent pastoral care. The choice of school is often a determining factor in where families choose to live within the borough, directly influencing property demand and values in different neighbourhoods.

Transport Links

Underground

The Piccadilly line serves three stations within the borough: Cockfosters (the line's northern terminus), Oakwood, and Southgate. This provides direct access to King's Cross St Pancras, Leicester Square, Piccadilly Circus, and Heathrow Airport. Journey times to Central London range from 25-35 minutes depending on the station. The Piccadilly line is one of the most significant factors supporting property values in the western part of the borough.

National Rail

Several National Rail stations serve the borough. The Great Northern line, operated by Great Northern (part of GTR), runs through Palmers Green, Winchmore Hill, Grange Park, and Gordon Hill, providing services to Moorgate via Finsbury Park. Journey times to Moorgate are approximately 25-30 minutes. The Liverpool Street line serves stations including Enfield Town, Edmonton Green, and Silver Street, with journey times of approximately 30 minutes to Liverpool Street.

Buses

Enfield has an extensive bus network operated by TfL, with routes connecting all parts of the borough to each other and to surrounding areas. Key routes include those along Green Lanes, Fore Street, and the A10, providing connections to underground stations and other transport hubs. Night bus services are more limited than in inner London but do serve some key routes through the borough.

Roads

The A10 (Great Cambridge Road) runs north-south through the borough, connecting to Central London and the M25. The A406 (North Circular) passes through the southern part of the borough near Edmonton. The M25 is accessible via junctions 24 (Cockfosters) and 25 (Enfield). While driving in the borough is generally easier than in inner London, congestion on the A10 and around Edmonton Green can be significant during peak hours.

Healthcare

Hospitals

North Middlesex University Hospital in Edmonton is the borough's main acute hospital, providing A&E, maternity, and a full range of hospital services. Chase Farm Hospital in Enfield, part of the Royal Free London NHS Foundation Trust, provides urgent care, outpatient services, and a range of specialist treatments following its redevelopment. For specialist care, residents also access services at the Royal Free Hospital in Hampstead and Barnet Hospital.

GP Services

Numerous GP practices operate across the borough, with most residents able to register with a practice within reasonable distance of their home. Some areas have experienced pressure on GP capacity due to population growth, but new facilities are being developed, particularly in Edmonton as part of the broader regeneration programme.

Parks and Green Spaces

Enfield is one of London's greenest boroughs, with extensive parks and open spaces. Trent Park (413 acres) in Cockfosters offers woodland walks, wildlife, and sporting facilities. Grovelands Park in Southgate features 90 acres of landscaped grounds. Forty Hall and Myddelton House Gardens provide heritage settings for outdoor enjoyment. The Lee Valley Regional Park runs along the borough's eastern boundary, offering cycling, walking, and water sports. For a borough within Greater London, the access to nature and green space is genuinely exceptional.

Local Amenities and Shopping

Enfield Town provides the borough's main shopping centre, with the Palace Exchange, Enfield Market, and a range of high street retailers. Palmers Green's Green Lanes offers independent shops and restaurants with a distinctive Mediterranean flavour. Southgate has a pleasant local shopping area around Southgate Circus. Edmonton Green shopping centre serves the southern part of the borough. For larger retail trips, Brent Cross Shopping Centre and Westfield Stratford are both accessible via public transport.
